blocks.content: Kit didn't move. He looked at the window at the end of the hall — the missing pane, the open square of daylight — and he waited.
A rock came through the window. It caught the man at the top of the stairs square in the shoulder — surprise more than pain, but loud.
Both men's eyes went to the window. Kit yanked his ankle free, levered himself up, and went down the remaining stairs like they were nothing.
Through the entry hall, out the front door, off the side of the porch — landed already running.
He came up breathing hard and nearly ran straight into Daisy. Three feet from the gap, still holding a second rock she hadn't needed.
Kit was scraped. His ankle throbbed. There was a piece of rotten wood still stuck in his shoelace.
Daisy punched him in the shoulder. Not hard — the kind that says something words aren't fast enough for.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: You scared me. You absolute jerk.
